vulnerability.reference: Clinicians exhibit automation bias, over-trusting AI-restored images without verifying against original degraded inputs.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians cannot distinguish hallucinated anatomical details from real pathology in AI-restored images, leading to over-reliance.

impact.reference: False anatomical structures in restored images lead to incorrect treatment decisions and patient harm.
impact.generated: Misdiagnosis and inappropriate medical interventions, causing patient harm.

dose_response.reference: SFBD-trained diffusion models' noise amplification hallucinates anatomical details in restored medical images; clinicians' automation bias converts these errors into misdiagnosis and patient harm.
dose_response.generated: Amplified noise patterns and distributional shift from SFBD-trained diffusion models, through clinicians and patients relying on AI-restored medical images, trigger the inability to distinguish hallucinated anatomical details from real pathology, leading to misdiagnosis and harmful interventions.
